demographics.living_situation: lives in a private residence; a neighbour is present and made the call

opening_complaint.verbatim_patient_text: Please help — my neighbour fell off a ladder and now he's gasping for air, clutching his chest, and his lips are completely blue! He can barely talk and he's going floppy.
opening_complaint.stated_reason_for_presenting_today: He fell about 3 metres onto his side around 15 minutes ago and he's getting worse really fast — he can't get his breath and now he's confused and slipping out of it.

symptom_narrative.onset: about 15 minutes ago, straight after he fell roughly 3 metres from a ladder onto his right side
symptom_narrative.duration: 15 minutes and getting worse every minute
symptom_narrative.character: he's fighting to breathe, gasping, and grabbing at the right side of his chest like it's agony
symptom_narrative.severity_patient_description: the worst thing I've ever seen — he can't say more than a word or two before running out of air
symptom_narrative.location_as_patient_describes: the right side of his chest
symptom_narrative.timing_pattern: constant and getting rapidly worse since the fall
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_worse: any movement and trying to breathe in makes the chest pain worse
symptom_narrative.associated_symptoms_volunteered: his lips and face have gone blue, he's drenched in sweat, he was agitated and confused and now he's starting to drift out of consciousness
symptom_narrative.previous_episodes: none that I know of — this all started with the fall
symptom_narrative.functional_impact: he's lying on the grass, can't get up, can't speak in sentences, barely conscious

social_history_volunteered.relevant_life_context: was up a ladder doing work at home when he fell; neighbour heard it and came over

objective_data_offered.value: his lips and face are completely blue
objective_data_offered.reliability_caveat: bystander description over the phone/video; not a measured oxygen level

objective_data_offered.value: he is gasping and can only get out one or two words before running out of breath
he's drenched in sweat, was agitated and confused, and is now drifting out of consciousness
he's clutching the right side of his chest and says it hurts too much to breathe in
a ladder is lying on the ground; he fell about 3 metres onto his right side roughly 15 minutes ago
